title: Understanding the Problem
text: The projectile is launched at an angle of $$30$$ degrees and lands $$20$$ seconds later at the same height. We need to find the initial speed.

title: Breaking Down the Motion
text: The motion can be broken down into horizontal and vertical components. Use the angle to find these components.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: The horizontal component of velocity is $$v cos\left(30\right)$$ and the vertical component is $$v sin\left(30\right)$$.

title: Time of Flight
text: The time of flight for a projectile is given by the formula: $$t=\frac{2v sin\left(\theta\right)}{g}$$, where g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Calculate Initial Speed
text: Using the time of flight formula, solve for the initial speed v.
